sections.paragraph:
ἀγρός, οῦ, ὁ (cp. ἄγω: DELG s.v. ἀγρός; Hom.+) field, land, countryside.

① open country as opposed to city or village, countryside, land, field Hv 2, 1, 4; 9:3 al. ἐν (τῷ) ἄγρῳ in the field (PAmh 134, 5; ‘[like a gazelle] in open country’ 2 Km 2:18; 10:8 al.) Mt 24:18; Lk 17:31; εἰς τὸν ἀγρόν in the field Mk 13:16; εἶναι ἐν (τῷ) ἀ. Mt 24:40; Lk 15:25; 17:35 v.l.; ἔρχεσθαι εἰς τὸν ἀ. go (out) into the country Hv 3, 1, 2; πορεύεσθαι εἰς ἀ. (Timaeus Hist. [IV/III b.c.]: 566 Fgm. 48, 2 Jac. [Athen. 12, 15, 518d]; Ruth 2:2) Mt 24:18; Mk 16:12 or ὑπάγειν εἰς ἀ. Hv 4, 1, 2; περιπατεῖν εἰς τὸν ἀ. Hs 2:1. ἔρχεσθαι ἀπʼ ἀγροῦ come in fr. the country Mk 15:21; Lk 23:26; εἰσέρχεσθαι ἐκ τοῦ ἀ. (cp. PEleph 13, 6 οὔπω εἰσελήλυθεν ἐξ ἀγροῦ; Gen 30:16; Jos., Ant. 5, 141) Lk 17:7 (s. B-D-F §255; Mlt. 82); cp. πάρεστιν ἀπʼ ἀγροῦ 11:6 D.—B. 1304.

② freq. in pl. property that is used for farming purposes, farm, estate (cp. Lat. ager=estate.—X., Mem. 3, 9, 11; SIG 914, 39; OGI 235, 2; 1 Km 8:14; 22:7 al.; cp. Josh 19:6; Jos., Ant. 17, 193) Mt 19:29; 22:5; Mk 10:29f; 14:18 (but s. 3); Lk 15:15. W. πόλις: ἀπήγγειλαν εἰς τὴν πόλιν καὶ εἰς τοὺς ἀγρούς among the farms Mk 5:14; Lk 8:34; w. κῶμαι (Dio Chrys. 13 [7], 42) Mk 6:36; Lk 9:12; w. κῶμαι and πόλεις Mk 6:56.

③ land put under cultivation, arable land, field (X., Mem. 1, 1, 8) Mt 13:24, 27, 31, 38; Lk 14:18 (s. 2); Ac 4:37; Hv 3, 1, 3. In it grow τὰ κρίνα τοῦ ἀ. wild lilies Mt 6:28; χόρτος τοῦ ἀ. (Gen 3:18; 4 Km 19:26) vs. 30; ζιζάνια τοῦ ἀ. weeds in the field 13:36; παμβότανον τοῦ ἀ. 1 Cl 56:14 (Job 5:25).—Used to hide treasure Mt 13:44; ἀ. τοῦ κεραμέως potter’s field 27:7f, 10 (s. GStrecker, Der Weg der Gerechtigkeit ’62, 76–82). KDieterich, RhM 59, 1904, 226ff.—M-M. TW.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Timaeus Timaeus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. fr. = from

PEleph PEleph = Elephantine-Papyri—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Mlt. Mlt. = JMoulton; with page number alone specified, this abbr. refers to A Grammar of NT Greek I: Prolegomena—List 6

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

  1. pl. = plural

Lat. Lat. = Latin

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Dio Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

RhM RhM = Rheinisches Museum für Philologie—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 15
